x-enumeration-literals.name: Market Value Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Value based on the current open market price of the asset.
Used for: Traded assets (stocks, bonds, commodities).

Comment:
Example: Listed shares valued at their latest exchange price.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Appraised Value Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Value determined by an independent professional appraiser.
Used for: Real estate, specialized or illiquid assets.

Comment:
Example: A house valued by a certified surveyor.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Book Value Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Value recorded in the owners accounting records.
Used for: Corporate assets, equipment.

Comment:
Example: Machinery valued at cost minus depreciation.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Forced Sale Value Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Estimated value if the asset must be sold quickly, often at a discount.
Used for: Risk and recovery scenarios.

Comment:
Example: Auction value of repossessed equipment.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Model Value Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Value calculated using a financial or statistical model.
Used for: Complex or illiquid instruments (derivatives, structured products).

Comment:
Example: Pricing a derivative using a valuation model.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Nominal Value Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Face or par value of the asset.
Used for: Simple instruments like bonds or guarantees.

Comment:
Example: A bond valued at its face value of €1,000.
